advisory.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a test that `run_git(..., text=False, input_data=b'...')` succeeds and forwards the raw bytes (e.g. hash-object --stdin returns the same hash as hashing the equivalent file, with bytes stdout).
evidence: gitutil.py:58 `if text and isinstance(input_data, bytes):` — guard fires ONLY when text is true
gitutil.py:56 docstring: "Binary stdin requires ``text=False`` (then stdout/stderr are ``bytes`` too)."
search for `text=False` in tests/unit: (no matches) — the bytes+text=False success path is uncovered
src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_ref_lock.py:282 uses `text=False` for binary git output, so the escape hatch is a real, used contract
Regression-blindness: mutating the guard to `if isinstance(input_data, bytes)` would leave the whole suite green
advisory.finding: The new guard is conditional (`if text and isinstance(input_data, bytes)`), carving out a documented escape hatch — bytes stdin with `text=False` must be forwarded as raw bytes — but no test exercises that positive branch. If the guard were widened to reject bytes unconditionally (dropping the `text and` predicate), every existing test would still pass while the documented binary-stdin contract silently breaks.
scenarios: A refactor drops the `text and` predicate, rejecting all bytes stdin
A caller passing bytes stdin with text=False (binary hash-object / cat-file) regresses undetected
advisory.suggested_fix: Add `test_bytes_input_with_text_false_is_forwarded`: pipe `input_data=b"hello from stdin\n"` to `git hash-object --stdin` with `text=False`, and assert the resulting (bytes) hash equals git hashing a file written with the same bytes.
